comment.body:
both of them, bcs both gamma and vega have reflexive effects on volatility.
vega on the longer term, gamma on the nearer term (like 4 weeks)
comment.raw_body:
both of them, bcs both gamma and vega have reflexive effects on volatility.

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
bcs if dealer is long vega, he will sell vol. 
this reflexively dumps the convexity of the skew at that certain segment, and so the gamma will increase there.
if gamma increases their, the local delta hedging flow counters the momentum, and what it causes, it compresses the daily returns.
and bcs volatility is the SD of the logreturns... it reflexively/directly compresses vol
comment.raw_body:
bcs if dealer is long vega, he will sell vol.

and bcs volatility is the SD of the logreturns... it reflexively/directly compresses vol

now, the vanna and speed positioning bets on the skewness of the distribution.
vanna is the vega vs spot (also delta vs vol, due to Schwarz's theorem),
and speed is the gamma vs spot

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
if customers long the iv skew, they long the right tail basically... they bet on upside vol.
they are long gamma and long vega to the upside, expecting convexity there.
dealers will be short gamma and short vega, so they will trade along with the momentum, creating illiquidity.
comment.raw_body:
if customers long the iv skew, they long the right tail basically... they bet on upside vol.
they are long gamma and long vega to the upside, expecting convexity there.
dealers will be short gamma and short vega, so they will trade along with the momentum, creating illiquidity.

meaning, they bet on downward liquidity.

comment.body: And lastly now, vomma

user.name: Alma
user.bio: Privat investor & speculator | Mathematics | Self-made quant | Market researcher | Options and Futures trader

comment.body:
vomma is nothing but the volatility of volatility. think about it. 
It is positive for long options across the strikes, mostly at the tails.
so whenever the customers short vomma, they sell the tails.
or they sell VIX vega, synthetically the same/similar.

both results in longer vega/gamma exposures for the dealers the tails,
so the momentum will face counterflow there, and volatility will be suppressed, reflexively.
comment.raw_body:
vomma is nothing but the volatility of volatility. think about it.

so whenever the customers short vomma, they sell the tails.

so the momentum will face counterflow there, and volatility will be suppressed, reflexively.
